items.name: Candidate dumps an OPTIONS PPM via env var
items.spec: Some clean mechanism (env var, CLI arg, etc.) makes the candidate harness compose+render the OPTIONS screen and write a 640x480 P6 PPM at /tmp/sdl3_dump/screen_00.ppm. Main-menu pathway must still work unchanged.

items.name: Composition correct (background + 4 buttons, no logo, no version)
items.spec: The dump shows: bank-6 idx-0 fullscreen background; four B196x33 buttons with text Controls / Display / Audio / Go Back; no bank-208 logo overlay; no version overlay. Per docs/design/screen-options.md.

items.name: Anchors correct (engine-native (-89, [-142,-90,-38,15]))
items.spec: Each button's on-screen position matches the engine's `(x=-89, y in {-142,-90,-38,15})` plus screen-center plus sprite offset, within ~5px of the reference. The button column is biased right of geometric center — matching the reference where the planet is visible to the right of the column.

items.name: Buttons rendered in INACTIVE state on dump (no Controls pulse)
items.spec: All four buttons render with `state=INACTIVE`, `effectbrightness=128`, `res_index=7`. The reference shows uniform brightness across all four buttons (no pulse on Controls despite engine `activeobject=0`); the candidate must match.

items.name: Visual equivalence to /tmp/real_options_dump.ppm
items.spec: Final gate. Candidate PPM is visually equivalent to /tmp/real_options_dump.ppm to a casual reader, modulo aliasing / blend-noise / star-RNG / animation-phase nits (<1% pixel difference acceptable). Anchors zero-offset where measurable; palette match; codec match; text legible.
